heading: Step 7: Edit the .
body:
- To add an IP (Internet Protocol) address or CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block from which you want
  Atlas to accept API (Application Programming Interface) requests for this service account, click
  **Add Access List Entry** and type an IP (Internet Protocol) address.

  You can also click **Use Current IP Address** if the host
  you are using to access Atlas also will make API (Application Programming Interface) requests
  using this service account.

- To remove an IP (Internet Protocol) address from the access list, click
  trash-alt to the right of the IP (Internet Protocol) address.

- Click **Save**.

You can use the Atlas Administration API to:

- [Update service account details](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updateorgserviceaccount).

- [Generate a new client secret](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-createorgserviceaccountsecret).

- [Update the service account's IP access list](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updateorgserviceaccount).

You can change the roles, description, or access list for an
API (Application Programming Interface) Key in an organization using the Atlas UI.

On the Add API Key page:

- Modify the Description.

- In the Organization Permissions menu, select the
  [organization role](organization-roles) for the API (Application Programming Interface) key.

* To add an IP (Internet Protocol) address or CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block from which you want
  Atlas to accept API (Application Programming Interface) requests for this API (Application Programming Interface) Key, click
  Add Access list Entry and type an IP (Internet Protocol) address.

  You can also click Use Current IP Address if the host
  you are using to access Atlas also will make API (Application Programming Interface) requests
  using this API (Application Programming Interface) Key.

* To remove an IP (Internet Protocol) address from the access list, click
   to the right of the IP (Internet Protocol) address.

* Click Save.

You can change the roles or access list for an API (Application Programming Interface) key in an
organization using the Atlas CLI.
